title: Request Context, Deadlines, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs request context, deadlines, cancellation, and cleanup from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace when an incoming request context is canceled and pass it through every request-scoped operation without storing it in long-lived structs.
Allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts.
Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Request Context Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
Order the Request Context Lifecycle Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Request Context Lifecycle.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Request Context Lifecycle assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Request Context Lifecycle, keep Request Context Lifecycle connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A review team handles case 4513 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace when an incoming request context is canceled and pass it through every request-scoped operation without storing it in long-lived structs.
